hypothesis: A persisted (invasive) edit fixes the immediate answer and degrades the next answer that attends over it; a non-invasive fork fixes the immediate answer without degrading the horizon.
pass_criterion: invasive d_acc1 CI95>0; invasive d_acc2 CI95<0; fork d_acc2 CI95 upper >= invasive d_acc2 upper
interpretation: If PASS: writing an intervention into the live stream buys the near token at the cost of the horizon, the KV-contamination failure; a fork-and-select avoids it. This is the case for non-invasive experimentation.
